items.title: Walmart flooded with RTX 40-series GPUs as 50-series remains out of reach for most gamers — retailer slashes up to $480 off last-gen GPUs to offer sensible prices
items.description: Walmart stocks up on Nvidia's GeForce RTX 40-series (codenamed Ada Lovelace) graphics cards amid an AI-driven memory shortage.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Daily tracking of GPU prices and stock for Tom's Hardware's GPU index has revealed a major influx of GeForce RTX 40-series (codename Ada Lovelace) graphics cards at Walmart. While Ada Lovelace cards may have slipped from the very top of the best graphics cards list, they remain competitive, especially at their new, sharply discounted prices. The PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Super Verto's price, for example, has plummeted from $1,501 to just $1,019 in the past 24 hours alone.Check out the PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 XLR8 Gaming Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4060 Ti Verto deal on WalmartFalling victim to the persistent memory shortage, Nvidia’s latest GeForce RTX 50-series (codenamed Blackwell) graphics cards continue to see substantial price hikes, effectively creating a big gap between them and gamers. The surges have shed a positive light on the previous generation of Ada Lovelace-powered graphics cards, which are becoming increasingly attractive options.One might even dare to say that Ada Lovelace is aging like fine wine amid current market dynamics. We wouldn't normally recommend purchasing last-generation graphics cards in normal circumstances. However, the current pricing landscape makes them a compelling alternative for desperate gamers seeking an immediate system upgrade, especially if you're rocking an older 30-series card, for instance. Walmart seems to share the same idea.The major U.S. retailer has significantly increased its inventory of 40-series graphics cards, likely in response to the high prices and limited availability of the newer Blackwell GPUs. New listings show a wide range of Ada Lovelace options from the GeForce RTX 4060 to the GeForce RTX 4080 Super. More importantly, Walmart has significantly lowered prices on Ada Lovelace-powered graphics cards by over $400 on some models. This strategy positions Walmart as the go-to retailer for those seeking value in the current volatile graphics card market.As to the retailers' specific motivations, there are a couple of possible factors. It may be that the AI crunch on PC components is making it harder for retailers to come by RTX 50-series GPUs, forcing them to turn to older models. It's also possible that Walmart has plentiful RTX 50-series stock, but that buyers are simply not parting with their cash because of increased prices. GPU sales generally may also be down as users avoid upgrading or building rigs due to inflated prices of other parts like SSDs or RAM. Whatever the reason, Walmart has decided now is the opportune time to get back into the RTX 40-series business.Nvidia GeForce RTX 40-Series Graphics Card PricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyGraphics CardMarch 18 Lowest U.S. PriceMarch 19 Lowest U.S. PricePrice DifferenceOriginal MSRPGeForce RTX 4090$3,199$3,199N/A$1,599GeForce RTX 4080 Super$1,501$1,019-$482$999GeForce RTX 4080$1,499$1,129-$370$1,199GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super$1,249$799-$450$799GeForce RTX 4070 Ti$796$739-$57$799GeForce RTX 4070 Super$898$579-$319$599GeForce RTX 4070$729$579-$150$549GeForce RTX 4060 Ti 16GBOOSOOSN/A$499GeForce RTX 4060 Ti 8GB$469$379-$90$399GeForce RTX 4060$413$435+$22$299The lowest price on a GeForce RTX 4080 Super yesterday was $1,501, but the influx of these graphics cards at Walmart has set a new lowest price of $1,019, resulting in a savings of $482. It's only $20 over the graphics card's original MSRP. We observed similar price cuts with other models. The GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super has dropped by $450, finding its original MSRP. Meanwhile, the GeForce RTX 4070 Super is $319 cheaper than yesterday's lowest price and $20 lower than its MSRP.The GeForce RTX 4070 Super is the outlier in the current lineup, as it retails at the same price as the GeForce RTX 4070, at least for today. In contrast, the GeForce RTX 4090 has remained consistently priced with no fluctuations, reflecting its premium status and steady demand. Surprisingly, the GeForce RTX 4060 has bucked expectations, rising in price rather than becoming more budget-friendly. It is just a small example of the unpredictable nature of today's graphics card market.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsFor example, the most affordable GeForce RTX 5070 on the market currently sells for $629, which is 9% more expensive than the GeForce RTX 4070 Super and delivers marginally better performance. There's also the topic of features, though. While Nvidia has expanded some of its technologies backwards to Ada Lovelace, such as DLSS 4.5, some features are only fully enabled on Blackwell. Take Multi-Frame Generation, which runs up to 6X on Blackwell but is restricted to 2X on these cards. A quick glance at our GPU hierarchy shows how these cards stack up against newer models for raw performance:Image 1 of 4(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)Shoppers have to weigh whether Blackwell's performance gains and exclusive gaming features justify its premium. But in the current situation, you can't deny that Ada Lovelace offers good value even if it's a previous-generation product. items.author: Zhiye Liu
items.titleZh: RTX 50系列显卡触不可及，沃尔玛RTX 40系列库存堆积如山——零售商最高降价480美元促销上代显卡
items.descriptionZh:
近期，全球显卡市场出现显著分化，引发消费者关注。Tom's Hardware的每日GPU价格与库存追踪数据显示，美国零售商沃尔玛正在大量补货英伟达基于Ada Lovelace架构的GeForce RTX 40系列显卡，并对其进行了大幅度的价格下调。这一动作与新一代基于Blackwell架构的RTX 50系列显卡因持续的内存短缺和AI领域需求挤压导致的严重供应紧张和价格飙升形成了鲜明对比。市场动态促使上一代产品重现高性价比吸引力。

**市场背景与价格动态**
当前，RTX 50系列显卡的高昂价格和有限供货在玩家与新一代产品之间制造了巨大鸿沟。这一情况反而让已上市的RTX 40系列显卡显得更具吸引力。沃尔玛显然抓住了这一市场机会，大幅增加了RTX 40系列的库存，并对多款型号进行了超过400美元的深度降价，旨在成为当前波动市场中寻求性价比用户的首选零售商。

具体的价格变动数据显示了此次降价的力度：
- **RTX 4080 Super**：最低价在24小时内从1501美元暴跌至1019美元，降幅达482美元，仅比其999美元的官方建议零售价（MSRP）高出20美元。
- **RTX 4070 Ti Super**：价格下降450美元，直接回落至其799美元的官方建议零售价。
- **RTX 4070 Super**：降价319美元，现价579美元，甚至比其599美元的官方建议零售价还低20美元。
- **RTX 4070**：价格降至579美元，与RTX 4070 Super当前价位相同。
- 其他型号如RTX 4080、RTX 4070 Ti等也有显著降价。
值得注意的是，旗舰型号RTX 4090价格保持稳定，体现了其高端市场的稳固需求。而RTX 4060则逆势小幅上涨，凸显了当前显卡市场的不可预测性。

**技术对比与购买考量**
从技术层面看，新一代Blackwell架构（RTX 50系列）虽然带来了性能提升和独家功能，但Ada Lovelace架构（RTX 40系列）仍具备强大竞争力。英伟达已将部分新技术，如DLSS 4.5，向后兼容至Ada Lovelace显卡，但某些核心功能，如“多帧生成”（Multi-Frame Generation），在Blackwell上可实现最高6倍的性能，而在Ada Lovelace上则被限制在2倍。

性能与价格比是当前消费者决策的关键。以市场上最便宜的RTX 5070为例，其当前售价为629美元，比降價后的RTX 4070 Super（579美元）贵约9%，但性能提升幅度有限。因此，对于迫切需要升级系统（特别是仍在使用的30系列或更早显卡的用户）的玩家而言，大幅降价的RTX 40系列提供了一个极具吸引力的替代选择。

**市场动因分析**
零售商此举的背后可能有多重因素：AI热潮对PC组件的挤压使得RTX 50系列GPU采购困难，迫使零售商转向旧型号；也可能沃尔玛虽有RTX 50系列库存，但因价格过高导致消费者购买意愿低迷；此外，SSD、内存等其他部件价格上涨也可能抑制了用户升级或组装整机的需求，导致GPU整体销量下滑。无论如何，沃尔玛判断现在是重新发力RTX 40系列业务的有利时机。

**总结**
在当前的市场环境下，尽管RTX 40系列已非最新一代，但其大幅降价后的价格与仍属主流的性能相结合，提供了出色的性价比。对于大多数游戏玩家来说，在Blackwell显卡价格回归理性之前，降价的Ada Lovelace显卡是一个务实且高价值的选择。沃尔玛的库存策略和定价调整，精准回应了由供应链问题和AI需求外溢导致的消费级显卡市场结构性变化。

items.title: Save $350 on the cheapest RTX 5070 Ti laptop with an OLED display — Acer's excellent Predator Helios Neo 16S AI with 32GB of RAM is just $1,549 right now
items.description: Powerful laptops with impressive specs are often overpriced and only make sense when on sale. Thankfully, Acer has put its Helios Neo 16S AI with an RTX 5070 Ti, 32 GB of RAM, 240 Hz OLED display, and a Core Ultra 9 275HX processor on a steep discount. It's the best Windows laptop you can likely find at this price.

items.titleZh: 宏碁掠夺者Helios Neo 16S AI笔记本直降350美元——配备OLED屏与32GB内存的最低价RTX 5070 Ti机型现售1549美元
items.descriptionZh:
在当前个人电脑市场中，组装台式机的内存等组件价格居高不下，使得游戏笔记本电脑成为愈发吸引人的选择，尤其对潜在的首台电脑组装者而言。我们注意到一款此类设备的优惠信息：宏碁（Acer）Predator Helios Neo 16S AI 游戏笔记本正在 Best Buy 限时促销，售价仅为 1,549 美元，较原价直降 350 美元。

这款笔记本不仅是该型号的历史最低价，同时也是市面上配备 OLED 显示屏的 RTX 5070 Ti 笔记本电脑中价格最优惠的机型。其核心硬件配置堪称旗舰级别：搭载了英特尔酷睿 Ultra 9 275HX 处理器，拥有 24 个核心，最高睿频可达 5.4 GHz，配合 32 GB 的 DDR5-6400 高速内存，足以流畅应对任何生产力任务。

显示方面，它采用了一块 16 英寸、刷新率高达 240 Hz 的 OLED 面板，支持 G-Sync 技术，并实现了 100% 的 DCI-P3 色域覆盖，能提供色彩鲜艳、响应迅速的视觉体验。

当进行游戏、渲染或视频编辑时，得益于 Helios 系列的散热系统，功耗 115W 的 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti 独立显卡能够释放出强劲性能。尽管高负载下风扇噪音较为明显，且内置扬声器不足以掩盖此噪声，但所有评测均指出其实际性能表现异常出色。就游戏本而言，它的设计相对轻薄，重量约 5 磅（约 2.27 公斤），厚度不足 19 毫米。

输入/输出接口配置全面且实用，包括多个高速 USB Type-A 和 Type-C 端口、耳机插孔、以太网口、HDMI 接口以及一个 microSD 读卡器。无线连接方面，支持蓝牙 5.4 和 Wi-Fi 6E。其内置的 76 Wh 电池可支持接近全天的移动使用。

总体而言，以 1,549 美元的价格，就能获得如此性能规格的宏碁 Predator Helios Neo 16S AI 游戏笔记本，无疑是一个非常超值的选择。文章最后还建议，若读者希望寻找更多优惠，可以参考网站的其他硬件deal页面，例如 SSD 和存储设备、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡、游戏椅、最佳 Wi-Fi 路由器、主板或 CPU 等专项优惠。

items.title: How Nvidia's $20 billion Groq 3 LPU deal reshapes the Nvidia Vera Rubin Platform — Samsung 4nm process serves as bedrock for SRAM-based AI accelerator chip
items.description: Nvidia unveiled the Groq 3 language processing unit at GTC 2026 in San Jose on Monday, marking the first chip to emerge from its $20 billion licensing and talent deal with Groq.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia unveiled the Groq 3 language processing unit at GTC 2026 in San Jose on Monday, marking the first chip to emerge from its $20 billion licensing and talent deal with AI inference startup Groq, which was struck on Christmas Eve last year. The SRAM-based inference accelerator slots into the Vera Rubin platform as a dedicated decode-phase co-processor, and Nvidia plans to ship it in Q3 2026, manufactured by Samsung on a 4nm process. It is the company's first rack-scale product built around non-GPU silicon — and its arrival has already displaced a homegrown Nvidia chip from the roadmap.The LP30 chip at the heart of the Groq 3 LPX rack carries 512 MB of on-chip SRAM per die, delivering 150 TB/s of memory bandwidth. That figure dwarfs the 22 TB/s available from the 288 GB of HBM4 on each Rubin GPU. A full LPX rack houses 256 LPUs for a total of 128GB of SRAM and 40 PB/s of aggregate bandwidth. Nvidia claims the LPX rack, paired with a Vera Rubin NVL72, delivers 35 times higher throughput per megawatt than Blackwell NVL72 alone for trillion-parameter models, at a target price point of $45 per million tokens.Groq 3 and Vera Rubin Nvidia detailed its entire seven-chip Rubin SuperPOD strategy at GTC 2026. (Image credit: Nvidia)Rubin GPUs handle the compute-intensive prefill phase of a query, processing long input contexts, while Groq LPUs take over the decode phase, generating output tokens at low latency. Nvidia's Dynamo orchestration platform manages the split across heterogeneous hardware, distributing workloads based on batch size and parallelism requirements.Article continues below You may like Nvidia Groq 3 LPU and Groq LPX racks join Rubin platform at GTC — SRAM-packed accelerator boosts 'every layer of the AI model on every token' Nvidia's $20 billion Groq IP deal bolsters AI market domination Nvidia removes Rubin CPX accelerators from its roadmap The original, pre-Nvidia Groq LPU design used a fixed Very Long Instruction Word (VLIW) pipeline and large on-chip SRAM pools, with the compiler pre-scheduling the entire execution path at compile time, which meant deterministic latency with no cache misses or stalls. These chips also demonstrated raw single-user token rates in the thousands per second, but the architecture's weakness was always capacity. At 230MB of SRAM per chip in prior generations, fitting even medium-sized models required high chip counts, and the architecture was initially designed for convolutional neural networks.The Groq LP30 addresses some of these limitations with 512 MB of SRAM per die and 1.23 FP8 PFLOPS of compute capability. Samsung has ramped production from roughly 9,000 wafers to about 15,000 wafers as output shifts from samples to commercial manufacturing, with AWS announcing at GTC that it will deploy Groq 3 LPUs alongside more than one million Nvidia GPUs as part of an expanded partnership.Beyond the LP30, a future LP35 will add NVFP4 support, aligning with the Rubin Ultra generation, and an LP40 is planned for the Feynman architecture cycle after that.Rubin CPX axed?One conspicuous absence from GTC was the Rubin CPX, a GDDR7-based inference accelerator announced in September 2025 as part of the Vera Rubin platform; it was absent from all keynote slides and received no stage time. It appears — though not officially confirmed — that the CPX has been removed from Nvidia’s roadmap entirely, replaced in the platform hierarchy by Groq 3 LPX.Rubin CPX was designed to use cheaper, more available GDDR7 memory to accelerate the context phase of inference at lower power. But the Groq LPU offers higher bandwidth without requiring large quantities of any external memory, which is ideal in a market where HBM supply remains constrained, and GDDR7 production is still scaling. Off-roadmap parts could still ship to customers who have already invested in CPX software optimization, but there’s a clear shift in priorities at Nvidia.There’s also an uncanny comparison between this and the Mellanox acquisition in 2019. That ended up turning Nvidia’s NVLink and InfiniBand technologies into foundational infrastructure for AI clusters. Groq appears to be following a similar trajectory, whereby start-up tech is absorbed into the platform as a permanent new architectural layer.Inference chip consolidationNvidia's Groq deal is the largest in a wave of inference-focused acquisitions that swept through the semiconductor industry in 2025. In June, AMD acquired the engineering team from Untether AI, a RISC-V inference chip developer, after the startup shut down, and Nvidia itself paid over $900 million for networking startup Enfabrica's team and IP in September. Meta acquired custom-chip startup Rivos in October, and Intel attempted to buy SambaNova for a reported $1.6 billion, but the talks collapsed; the two companies settled on a $350 million investment and multi-year partnership last month instead. What to read next Examining Nvidia's 60 exaflop Vera Rubin POD — how seven chips underpin company's 40 rack AI factory supercomputer Nvidia buys AI chip rival Groq's IP for $20 billion in its biggest deal ever Nvidia launches Vera Rubin NVL72 AI supercomputer at CES There’s a consistent pattern here, where independent inference chip startups are being absorbed by large incumbents, and the economics of competing independently against Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem have proven unsustainable regardless of technical merit. Groq itself was targeting $500 million in revenue for the fiscal year 2025, but even that wasn’t enough to sustain independence. Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon noted in a research report that the deal's non-exclusive licensing structure may keep "the fiction of competition alive" while effectively neutralizing a rival.Hyperscaler custom silicon Meta outlined its MTIA AI accelerator roadmap just last week. (Image credit: Meta)While startups consolidate into incumbents, the hyperscalers are building their own inference hardware at an accelerating pace.Meta announced four successive MTIA chip generations on March 11, all developed in partnership with Broadcom: the MTIA 300 (already in production for ranking and recommendation training), MTIA 400 (completing lab testing), MTIA 450, and MTIA 500, with the latter two targeted at generative AI inference and scheduled for mass deployment in 2027. The company has already deployed hundreds of thousands of earlier MTIA chips for inference across its apps. From MTIA 300 to 500, HBM bandwidth increases 4.5 times, and compute FLOPS increase 25 times.Google's Ironwood TPU v7 delivers 4,614 TFLOPS per chip with 192 GB of HBM per die, scaling to 42.5 exaflops in 9,216-chip pods. AWS continues developing Trainium and Inferentia, though internal data reported in 2024 showed Trainium at just 0.5% of Nvidia GPU usage within AWS and Inferentia at 2.7%, suggesting adoption has lagged.Spending figures also demonstrate this diversification, with a Futurum Group survey from November 2025 finding that XPU accelerators are expected to lead data center compute spending growth at 22% in 2026, outpacing GPUs at 19% and CPUs at 14%. Meanwhile, TrendForce projects custom ASIC shipments from cloud providers growing 44.6% in 2026, compared to 16.1% growth for GPU shipments.Nvidia's response is to make sure its platform includes non-GPU silicon before someone else's does, and the Groq 3 LPU is the product of that. Meanwhile, the future of Rubin CPX appears uncertain, at least for now.
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 英伟达200亿美元Groq 3 LPU交易如何重构Vera Rubin平台——三星4纳米工艺为SRAM架构AI加速芯片奠定基石
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年3月于圣何塞举行的GTC大会上，英伟达正式发布了Groq 3语言处理单元，这是该公司自去年圣诞节前夕以200亿美元收购AI推理初创公司Groq的知识产权和人才后推出的首款芯片产品。这款基于SRAM的推理加速器将作为专用解码阶段协处理器，集成于Vera Rubin平台。英伟达计划于2026年第三季度开始出货，芯片由三星采用4纳米工艺制造。这是英伟达首款围绕非GPU芯片构建的机架级产品，它的出现甚至导致英伟达自家的一款芯片从路线图中被移除。

**核心技术原理与架构创新**

Groq 3 LPU的核心是LP30芯片，每颗裸片集成512 MB的片上SRAM，提供高达150 TB/s的内存带宽。这一数字远超每颗Rubin GPU上288 GB HBM4所能提供的22 TB/s带宽。一个完整的LPX机架容纳256颗LPU，总计拥有128GB SRAM和40 PB/s的聚合带宽。

其工作流程体现了异构计算分工：Rubin GPU负责处理计算密集型的预填充阶段，解析长输入上下文；而Groq LPU则接管解码阶段，以低延迟生成输出词元。英伟达的Dynamo编排平台负责管理这种异构硬件间的任务分割，根据批量大小和并行性需求分发工作负载。

此次发布的Groq LP30芯片是对原始Groq LPU设计的重大演进。原始设计采用固定的超长指令字流水线和大型片上SRAM池，编译器在编译时预先调度整个执行路径，从而实现确定性延迟，避免了缓存未命中或停滞。该架构曾展示出每秒数千词元的原始单用户吞吐率，但其核心弱点始终是容量。前几代芯片每颗仅230MB SRAM，即使部署中等规模模型也需要大量芯片，且其架构最初是为卷积神经网络设计的。

LP30通过将每裸片SRAM增至512 MB，并具备1.23 FP8 PFLOPS的计算能力，部分解决了这些限制。三星已将其产量从约9000片晶圆提升至约15000片，标志着生产从样品阶段转向商业制造。AWS在GTC上宣布，作为扩大合作的一部分，将在其云平台部署Groq 3 LPU， alongside 超过一百万颗英伟达GPU。

未来，英伟达还规划了支持NVFP4、与Rubin Ultra世代对齐的LP35，以及面向后续Feynman架构周期的LP40。

**性能参数与市场定位**

英伟达声称，一个LPX机架与一个Vera Rubin NVL72配对，在运行万亿参数模型时，每兆瓦的吞吐量比单独的Blackwell NVL72高出35倍，目标定价为每百万词元45美元。

这一性能提升主要得益于SRAM架构带来的极高带宽和低延迟，尤其适合对延迟敏感的大模型推理场景。

**产品路线图调整与行业影响**

一个显著的变动是，此前于2025年9月作为Vera Rubin平台一部分发布的、基于GDDR7内存的推理加速器Rubin CPX，在本次GTC大会的主题演讲和幻灯片中完全消失。虽然没有官方确认，但迹象表明CPX可能已完全从英伟达路线图中移除，被Groq 3 LPX所取代。

Rubin CPX原本旨在利用更便宜、更易获取的GDDR7内存，以更低功耗加速推理的上下文阶段。但Groq LPU提供了更高的带宽，且无需依赖大量外部内存，这在HBM供应持续紧张、GDDR7产能仍在爬升的市场中显得更为理想。尽管已投资CPX软件优化的客户可能仍会获得供货，但英伟达的优先级发生了明确转变。

这一收购整合模式令人联想到英伟达2019年对Mellanox的收购，后者最终将NVLink和InfiniBand技术打造成了AI集群的基础设施。Groq似乎正沿着相似的轨迹发展，其初创技术被吸收进平台，成为一个永久性的新架构层。

**AI推理芯片行业整合趋势**

英伟达收购Groq是2025年半导体行业一波以推理为核心的收购潮中规模最大的一笔。同年，AMD收购了已关闭的RISC-V推理芯片开发商Untether AI的工程团队；英伟达以超9亿美元收购了网络初创公司Enfabrica的团队和IP；Meta收购了定制芯片初创公司Rivos；英特尔曾试图以约16亿美元收购SambaNova，但谈判破裂，最终双方于上月达成了3.5亿美元的投资和多年期合作伙伴关系。

这一系列事件呈现出清晰模式：独立的推理芯片初创公司正被大型现有企业吸收。无论技术优势如何，独立对抗英伟达CUDA生态系统的经济模式已被证明难以为继。Groq本身在2025财年目标营收为5亿美元，但这仍不足以维持其独立运营。正如伯恩斯坦分析师Stacy Rasgon指出的，此次交易的非独家授权结构可能在“维持竞争表象”的同时，有效消除了一位竞争对手。

**超大规模企业的自定义芯片竞赛**

在初创公司被整合的同时，超大规模云服务商正在加速构建自己的推理硬件。

Meta于3月11日公布了与博通合作开发的四代MTIA AI加速器路线图：已用于排名和推荐训练的MTIA 300、已完成实验室测试的MTIA 400，以及针对生成式AI推理、计划于2027年大规模部署的MTIA 450和MTIA 500。该公司已在旗下应用中部署了数十万颗早期的MTIA芯片进行推理。从MTIA 300到500，HBM带宽增加了4.5倍，计算FLOPS提升了25倍。

谷歌的Ironwood TPU v7每芯片提供4,614 TFLOPS算力，每裸片配备192 GB HBM，可扩展至9216芯片集群，达42.5百亿亿次浮点运算。AWS则继续开发Trainium和Inferentia，不过2024年的内部数据显示，Trainium在AWS内部的GPU使用量中仅占0.5%，Inferentia占2.7%，表明其采用率相对滞后。

支出数据也印证了这种多元化趋势。Futurum Group于2025年11月的一项调查发现，预计2026年数据中心计算支出增长将由XPU加速器引领，增幅达22%，超过GPU的19%和CPU的14%。同时，TrendForce预计，2026年云提供商的自定义ASIC出货量将增长44.6%，而GPU出货量增长为16.1%。

英伟达的应对策略是确保自己的平台率先纳入非GPU芯片，Groq 3 LPU正是这一战略的产物。与此同时，Rubin CPX的未来，至少目前看来，已充满不确定性。这场由大模型推理需求驱动的芯片架构革新与行业格局重塑，正在持续加速演进。

items.title: AMD and Samsung ink memory supply memorandum for EPYC and Instinct products — unprecedented deal also includes scope for foundry partnership
items.description: Samsung to remain primary HBM memory supplier for AMD's AI accelerators as the companies look into possible foundry relationship.

Subscribe to our newsletter AMD and Samsung this week signed a rather unprecedented memorandum of understanding (MOU) that involves the strategic supply of memory for AMD's next-generation EPYC and Instinct MI455X products, as well as discussions of a potential foundry partnership. The MOU aims to ensure that AMD gets enough memory for its next-generation CPU and AI accelerator products throughout their life span, whereas Samsung will get a chance to serve as a foundry for a leading supplier of CPUs, AI GPUs, and other products.Samsung has been the primary supplier of HBM3E for AMD's Instinct MI350X and MI355X AI accelerators, so it is not surprising that the core of the new agreement is Samsung’s role as a primary supplier of HBM4 memory for AMD's upcoming Instinct MI455X accelerator. As demand for all types of memory in general and HBM4 in particular exceeds supply, AMD must ensure that its next-generation MI455X accelerator (and possibly other Instinct MI400-series products) gets enough memory.AMD's Instinct MI455X is projected to use 12 HBM4 12-Hi memory stacks for a total of 432 GB. Given such demands, it makes great sense for AMD to sign a strategic memory supply deal, though for now the two companies limited themselves to the MOU. Meanwhile, Samsung said that its HBM4 memory stacks are based on memory devices produced using its 1c (6th Generation 10nm-class) process technology and use a base die made on a 4nm-class logic process, which enables them to achieve a rather unprecedented data transfer rate of 13 GT/s (thus provide up to 3.3 TB/s of bandwidth per stack), which is well above JEDEC's recommended 8 GT/s for HBM4. Yet, memory speeds supported by Instinct MI455X are considerably slower.Article continues below You may like Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron team up to block memory hoarding Micron secures $318 million Taiwanese subsidy for HBM R&D as AI memory arms race intensifies Intel is co-developing new Z-Angle Memory to compete with HBM used in AI data centers In addition to HBM4, the MOU between the two companies also includes DDR5 supply for AMD's 6th Generation EPYC processors codenamed 'Venice,' as well as AMD's Helios rack-scale systems for AI data centers that rely on EPYC 'Venice' and Instinct MI455X. Again, the move aims to ensure a steady memory supply for next-generation AI systems running AMD's CPUs and AI accelerators to ensure a competitive position against Nvidia's Rubin-powered machines.Perhaps the most intriguing part of the announcement is that the MOU can open the door for a potential foundry relationship, under which Samsung could manufacture future AMD products. While no specific nodes or chips were disclosed, this signals a willingness to expand cooperation into logic production alongside memory supply. To put this into context, AMD has been drifting away from GlobalFoundries since late 2018, and by now virtually all of its advanced products are made at TSMC. Shifting certain advanced production to Samsung is costly, though if this ensures that the South Korean company will provide much-needed memory to support AMD's AI efforts, then it looks like the developer of EPYC CPUs and Instinct accelerators may be willing to adopt a dual-source foundry strategy and make at least some of its products at Samsung Foundry."Samsung and AMD share a commitment to advancing AI computing, and this agreement reflects the growing scope of our collaboration," said Young Hyun Jun, Vice Chairman & CEO of Samsung Electronics. "From industry-leading HBM4 and next-generation memory architectures to cutting-edge foundry and advanced packaging, Samsung is uniquely positioned to deliver unrivaled turnkey capabilities that support AMD’s evolving AI roadmap." items.titleZh: AMD与三星签署EPYC和Instinct产品存储供应备忘录——此次前所未有的协议还涵盖代工合作可能
items.descriptionZh:
本周，AMD与三星签署了一份意义深远的谅解备忘录（MOU），其核心内容包括为AMD下一代EPYC处理器及Instinct MI455X加速器提供战略性内存供应，并探讨潜在代工合作伙伴关系。此举旨在确保AMD下一代CPU和AI加速器产品在整个生命周期内获得充足的内存供应，而三星则有望成为这家领先CPU、AI GPU供应商的代工合作伙伴。

作为AMD Instinct MI350X和MI355X AI加速器HBM3E内存的主要供应商，三星在此次新协议中继续扮演关键角色，将成为AMD即将推出的Instinct MI455X加速器所需HBM4内存的主要供应商。当前，各类内存（尤其是HBM4）需求远超供应，AMD必须确保其下一代MI455X加速器（可能还包括其他Instinct MI400系列产品）获得足够的内存。预计MI455X将采用12个HBM4 12-Hi内存堆栈，总容量达432GB。面对如此巨大的需求，AMD签署战略性内存供应协议合情合理，不过目前双方仅停留在谅解备忘录阶段。

三星表示，其HBM4内存堆栈基于采用1c（第六代10nm级）工艺技术生产的存储芯片，并使用了4nm级逻辑工艺制造的基板芯片。这使得其数据传输速率达到了前所未有的13 GT/s（因此每堆栈可提供高达3.3 TB/s的带宽），远超JEDEC为HBM4推荐的8 GT/s标准。然而，Instinct MI455X实际支持的内存速度要慢得多。

除了HBM4，此次谅解备忘录还包括为AMD代号“威尼斯”的第六代EPYC处理器，以及依赖EPYC “威尼斯”和Instinct MI455X的AMD赫利奥斯AI数据中心机架级系统供应DDR5内存。此举同样是为了确保运行AMD CPU和AI加速器的下一代AI系统拥有稳定的内存供应，从而在与英伟达基于Rubin平台的系统的竞争中保持有利地位。

此次声明中最引人关注的部分或许是，该谅解备忘录可能为潜在的代工合作关系打开大门，即三星未来可能为AMD制造产品。尽管未披露具体的工艺节点或芯片，但这表明双方有意愿将合作从内存供应扩展到逻辑芯片制造领域。回溯背景，AMD自2018年底以来已逐渐与格芯疏远，目前其几乎所有先进产品均由台积电制造。将部分先进生产转向三星成本高昂，但如果此举能确保这家韩国公司为AMD的AI战略提供急需的内存，那么这位EPYC CPU和Instinct加速器的开发商似乎愿意采用双源代工策略，并至少在三星代工厂生产部分产品。

三星电子副董事长兼首席执行官Young Hyun Jun表示：“三星和AMD都致力于推进AI计算，此协议反映了我们不断扩大的合作范围。从行业领先的HBM4和下一代内存架构，到尖端的代工和先进封装技术，三星拥有独特的优势，能够提供全面的交钥匙能力，支持AMD不断演进的AI路线图。”

items.title: Kioxia announces new Super High IOPS SSD that helps accelerate AI workloads on Nvidia GPUs — 25.6TB drive provides more GPU-accessible memory for faster data access
items.description: Kioxia has developed a new AI SSD that is designed to provide a secondary cache for Nvidia AI GPUs. The new drive comes with the manufacturer's XL-Flash that is SLC based and performs at over 10 million IOPS.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia and Kioxia have been working on a new SSD design that will keep Nvidia's AI GPUs fed with no downtime during intensive AI workloads. Kioxia has announced the new E3.S CM9 series, which Kioxia classifies as a "Super High IOPS" SSD that offers 25.6TB of capacity and can write to itself three times per day for the entirety of its warranty period. The new drive will be available for customers to evaluate by the end of 2026.The new drive is part of Kioxia and Nvidia's move to bring incredibly fast storage to AI GPUs. The CM9 series comes with Kioxia's XL-flash, which is designed to achieve over 10 million IOPS, a figure that is around three to four times greater than traditional datacenter SSDs. XL-Flash is made using SLC NAND flash (the fastest flash type available) that boasts a read latency range of just 3 to 5 microseconds. By comparison, traditional SSDs normally have a peak at 3 to 4 million IOPS and have read latencies in the 40 to 100-microsecond range.These new drives are part of Nvidia's Storage-Next design that sees servers utilize these drives in conjunction with a direct link to the GPU itself, bypassing additional latency penalties that would occur when transferring data from the CPU. This technology is designed to offset the limitations of existing HBM memory, giving Nvidia's AI GPUs a secondary cache layer to store data and keep the GPU cores operating at 100% without any downtime. This addresses the growing problem of growing AI models that are scaling towards trillions of parameters and context windows that consist of millions of tokens.Article continues below You may like First PCIe 6.0 SSD enters mass production with 28GB/s speeds, 5.5 million IOPS, and liquid cooling SK hynix and SanDisk announce speedy HBF flash chip standard Phison demos 10X faster AI inference on consumer PCs with software and hardware combo that enables 3x larger AI models We first saw this technology with the BlueField-4 STX storage architecture at GTC 2026. BlueField-4 uses a storage-optimized BlueField-4 DPU and ConnectX-9 SuperNIC that Nvidia claims delivers up to five times the token throughput, four times better energy efficiency, and twice the page ingestion speed compared to traditional CPU-based storage architectures. items.titleZh: 铠侠发布新款超高IOPS固态硬盘 加速英伟达GPU人工智能负载——25.6TB容量提供更多GPU可访问内存 实现更快数据读取
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达与铠侠正合作开发一款新型SSD，旨在为高强度AI工作负载中的英伟达AI GPU提供持续无间断的数据供给。铠侠近日宣布了E3.S CM9系列，该系列被归类为“超高IOPS”SSD，提供25.6TB容量，并保证在其整个保修期内每天可完成三次全盘写入。新驱动器预计将于2026年底可供客户评估。

这款新驱动器是铠侠与英伟达推动为AI GPU提供极速存储解决方案的一部分。CM9系列搭载了铠侠的XL-Flash技术，旨在实现超过1000万IOPS，这一数字约为传统数据中心SSD的三到四倍。XL-Flash采用SLC NAND闪存（目前最快的闪存类型），其读取延迟范围仅为3至5微秒。相比之下，传统SSD的峰值IOPS通常在300万至400万，读取延迟在40至100微秒范围内。

这些新驱动器属于英伟达“Storage-Next”设计的一部分。在该设计中，服务器将这些驱动器与GPU本身直接链接，绕过了通过CPU传输数据时产生的额外延迟开销。此项技术旨在弥补现有HBM内存的容量限制，为英伟达的AI GPU提供一个二级缓存层来存储数据，从而确保GPU核心能够保持100%利用率，无需任何停机等待。这解决了日益增长的AI模型规模所带来的难题——模型参数量正朝向万亿级发展，上下文窗口也包含数百万个令牌。

我们最早在2026年GTC大会上通过BlueField-4 STX存储架构见证了此项技术。BlueField-4采用了一个面向存储优化的BlueField-4 DPU和ConnectX-9 SuperNIC。英伟达宣称，与传统的基于CPU的存储架构相比，该架构可提供高达5倍的令牌吞吐量、4倍的能效提升以及2倍的页面摄取速度。

items.title: Intel's new feature can improve game loading times by up to 3x — Precompiled Shader Delivery comes to Arc Xe2 and Xe3 GPUs following DirectX SDK release
items.description: Following in Nvidia's footsteps, Intel has now officially adopted Microsoft's Advanced Shader Delivery to make shader compilation much faster in games. Intel is calling it Precompiled Shader Distribution and it's available on a bunch of Arc GPUs right away, supported in 11 games at launch, with more likely to follow. AMD is now the only company left who hasn't officially embraced this feature.

Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft launched Advanced Shader Delivery (ASD) last year as part of the DirectX SDK, but it was limited to only the ROG Xbox Ally devices at the time. The feature was always intended to eventually go mainstream — and Intel has just released a new graphics driver that brings a version of it to Arc GPUs. The company is calling it "Precompiled Shader Distribution" and it's available on select mobile and desktop chips.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCPrecompiled Shader Distribution downloads the shaders for a game ahead of time, so you don't have to wait for the shader cache to build up when you first open it. The process happens automatically in the background once the Intel Graphics app detects you have the game installed. Depending on your exact hardware config, it starts downloading shaders from Intel's cloud and loads them into a dedicated folder.Intel told TechPowerUp it is "also working with Microsoft on launching Advanced Shader Delivery later this year," meaning that this is Intel's own implementation for now — it will only get better from here (hopefully). The feature is launching with 13 Steam games: They're all heavy-hitters and will offer some level of faster loading, while reducing shader stuttering mid-gameplay.Black Myth: WukongBorderlands 4Call of Duty: Black Ops 6Call of Duty: Black Ops 7Cyberpunk 2077God of War RagnarökGotham KnightsHogwarts LegacyNBA 2K26StarfieldS.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2: Heart of ChornobylThe Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion RemasteredThe Outer Worlds 2The company is claiming this will make your games load 2x faster on Arc B-series discrete GPUs and on the Core Ultra 200 series' integrated graphics, on average. The latest Panther Lake lineup with Xe3 graphics will benefit even more and will receive an average 3x improvement in game loading times. There's no support for Arc Alchemist yet — and it may not be coming soon, since Intel lists the latest Arc GPUs as a hardware requirement.Article continues below You may like Shader Execution Reordering part of new DirectX update boosts FPS by 90% in Intel Arc B-series GPUs Intel enables XeSS 3 Multi-Frame Generation in latest drivers, expanding frame generation across Arc GPUs and Core Ultra iGPUs Intel releases XeSS 3.0 SDK with 3x and 4x multi-frame gen support, but it's still not open source In the benchmarks shared, God of War: Ragnarok was the most impressive by far, loading an insane 37 times faster with Precompiled Shader Distribution on an Arc B390 iGPU. The Oblivion Remaster only saw a 1.3x improvement across both the Arc B580 graphics card and the Arc 140V iGPU on a Core Ultra 9 288V. Of course, not every game will take full advantage of this feature — especially not right away.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Intel)(Image credit: Intel)(Image credit: Intel)But as Advanced Shader Delivery becomes commonplace and other chipmakers like Nvidia and AMD started introducing it on the driver level, shader caching issues should slowly become a thing of the past. PC will be able to achieve parity with console in yet another field, since its plus point — the variety of hardware configs — has actually held back precompiled shaders from being a possibility on the platform for a long time.Precompiled Shader Delivery will also update cache for all the shaders alongside new drivers, so your game is always running smoothly. Within the driver release, Intel actually calls this feature "Graphics Shader Distribution Service" — but it refers to the same thing. If you want to enable it on your own Arc GPU, open the Intel Graphics Software, go to Graphics, then 3D Rendering, and you'll see the toggle right there. items.titleZh: 英特尔新功能可将游戏加载速度提升三倍——DirectX SDK发布后预编译着色器交付技术将登陆Arc Xe2和Xe3 GPU
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔近日通过新版图形驱动程序，为部分Arc GPU引入了名为“预编译着色器分发”（Precompiled Shader Distribution）的新功能。该技术源于微软在DirectX SDK中推出的“高级着色器交付”（Advanced Shader Delivery，ASD），去年首次在ROG Ally等Xbox设备上亮相，如今英特尔将其以自有方案形式带到了PC平台。

这项功能的核心原理是：在玩家首次运行游戏前，通过英特尔图形应用自动检测已安装的游戏，并从云端提前下载预编译好的着色器文件，存入专用文件夹。这避免了传统方式下，游戏首次启动时需即时编译着色器而导致的漫长等待和游戏中可能出现的着色器卡顿问题。英特尔表示，正在与微软合作，计划在今年晚些时候正式推出官方的Advanced Shader Delivery支持，当前的实现是英特尔的先行方案。

目前该功能首发支持13款Steam游戏，均为对硬件要求较高的大作，包括：《黑神话：悟空》、《无主之地4》、《使命召唤：黑色行动6》、《使命召唤：黑色行动7》、《赛博朋克2077》、《战神：诸神黄昏》、《哥谭骑士》、《霍格沃茨之遗》、《NBA 2K26》、《星空》、《潜行者2：切尔诺贝利之心》、《上古卷轴IV：湮没 重制版》以及《外部世界2》。

根据英特尔公布的数据，在Arc B系列独立显卡和酷睿Ultra 200系列集成显卡上，启用此功能后游戏加载速度平均可提升2倍。即将推出的、搭载Xe3核显的Panther Lake处理器预计将获得平均3倍的加载速度提升。需要注意的是，初代Arc Alchemist显卡目前暂不支持此功能，英特尔将最新Arc GPU列为硬件要求，暗示旧款支持可能不会很快到来。

性能基准测试显示，效果因游戏而异。在Arc B390集成显卡上运行《战神：诸神黄昏》时，加载速度提升了惊人的37倍。而在酷睿Ultra 9 288V处理器的Arc 140V核显及Arc B580独立显卡上运行《上古卷轴IV：湮没 重制版》时，提升幅度则为1.3倍。这表明并非所有游戏都能立即完全受益于该技术。

该服务会随着新驱动程序的发布而更新所有游戏的着色器缓存，确保游戏始终能流畅运行。在驱动程序内部，此功能被称为“图形着色器分发服务”（Graphics Shader Distribution Service）。用户可以在英特尔图形控制软件中启用它：打开Intel Graphics Software，进入“图形”设置，选择“3D渲染”，即可找到对应的开关。

从行业影响看，随着Advanced Shader Delivery的普及，以及未来英伟达和AMD等厂商在驱动层面引入类似技术，长期困扰PC游戏的着色器编译卡顿问题有望逐渐成为历史。这将使PC平台在保持硬件配置多样性的优势同时，弥补其在着色器预编译方面的传统短板，从而在游戏体验的另一个维度上向游戏主机看齐。

items.titleZh: 基于重放的验证：面向芯粒系统的可扩展方法学（英特尔、新思科技）
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔、英伟达与新思科技的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《采用回放驱动仿真与模拟的基于ODIN的CPU-GPU架构》的技术论文。这项研究针对现代计算系统，特别是基于芯粒（Chiplet）的CPU-GPU异构架构，提出了一种创新的、可扩展的预硅芯片验证方法。

**背景与挑战**
随着人工智能和图形工作负载的复杂性激增，将控制导向的CPU处理能力与大规模并行的GPU计算能力深度融合，已成为提升系统性能的关键。业界正朝着基于芯粒的模块化架构演进，这种架构虽然提高了设计灵活性和效率，但也让系统级验证变得极其复杂。传统的预硅验证方法在应对包含多个异构计算芯粒的完整系统时，往往面临周期长、成本高、难以覆盖真实应用场景的挑战。

**核心技术原理：回放驱动的验证方法**
论文提出的核心创新是一种“回放驱动”的验证方法。该方法基于“ODIN”框架，其核心思想是将从实际软件工作负载中捕获的“痕迹”或“执行流”，在硬件仿真或模拟平台中进行精确的回放。
1.  **工作负载捕获**：首先在参考平台或早期硅片上运行具有代表性的AI、图形或高性能计算应用，并记录下关键的CPU指令流、GPU计算任务、内存访问模式以及芯粒间通信数据。
2.  **在仿真环境中回放**：随后，将这些捕获到的“痕迹”输入到正在开发中的芯粒系统（如新的CPU、GPU或整个芯粒互连网络）的仿真模型中。系统模型会严格按照痕迹执行，重现真实的工作负载行为。
3.  **与参考结果比对**：通过对比回放结果与最初捕获的参考结果，验证人员可以高效、精确地检测出目标硬件设计在功能、性能和互连方面的任何偏差或错误。

**性能优势与可扩展性**
这种方法的显著优势在于其**可扩展性和效率**。
*   **脱离软件依赖**：验证过程无需在仿真环境中完整地重新运行庞大的操作系统和应用程序栈，极大降低了仿真复杂度和资源消耗。
*   **精准与可重复**：回放特定的工作负载痕迹使得测试用例高度可控且可重复，便于快速定位和调试问题。
*   **并行验证**：尤其适合芯粒架构，可以对单个芯粒或互连子系统进行独立的回放验证，然后再进行系统集成验证，加快了整体验证进度。
*   **加速验证周期**：相比完全在仿真中从头运行全栈软件，回放方法能显著缩短验证时间，使设计团队能更早、更频繁地进行系统级测试。

**技术影响与应用场景**
这项研究对推动高性能异构计算，特别是芯粒生态系统的发展具有重要影响。
*   **赋能芯粒互连**：它为验证像UCIe这样的开放芯粒互连标准在复杂CPU-GPU系统中的实际表现提供了强大工具，确保不同厂商生产的芯粒能够可靠地协同工作。
*   **支持敏捷开发**：使得在硬件原型可用之前就能进行深入的系统级性能和功能分析，支持更敏捷的硬件-软件协同设计。
*   **关键应用领域**：该方法直接适用于需要强大CPU-GPU协同的领域，例如人工智能训练与推理、高保真图形渲染、科学计算以及数据中心级加速。

总之，这项由行业领先企业合作提出的“回放驱动验证”方法论，为解决下一代芯粒化CPU-GPU系统所面临的严峻验证挑战，提供了一条高效、可扩展的路径，有助于加速复杂异构计算平台的上市进程并确保其可靠性。

items.title: With H200s set to flow into China, Groq is reportedly set to follow — Nvidia is allegedly preparing a custom version of inferencing chip to penetrate region
items.description: According to a new report, Nvidia's Groq inferencing chips are set to head to China for export, following approval of its H200 AI accelerators.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The silicon silk road to China is open once again: Beijing has given full approval for Nvidia to sell its H200 last-generation GPUs in the region. This follows months of back-and-forth talks between the U.S. government, Nvidia, and China, and though the Trump administration approved Nvidia sales, the Chinese government still needed to give the final nod.Now that it has it, Nvidia is gearing up for a big sales pitch to Chinese companies, Reuters reports, and not just for its H200 GPU. CEO Jensen Huang has said it has already received orders for the H200 chips, but it's arguably the Groq custom AI inferencing hardware that Nvidia is more keen to sell. After making a $14 billion deal with Groq, which develops custom AI ASICs — known as Language Processing Units (LPUs) — Nvidia wants to make a return on its investment. Can the company inject itself into the burgeoning market for Chinese inferencing chips?An oldie but a goodie The H200 isn't the latest in its AI data center efforts, but it is highly desirable for training LLMs compared to Chinese counterparts. (Image credit: Nvidia)On the enterprise front, Nvidia had to restart the production line for its H200 chips, having spent much of the past 12 months pushing its successor, the B300, based on their newer Blackwell architecture, the same one inside RTX 50-series GPUs. The company ultimately wound down H200 production because of the regulatory hurdles it was forced to jump through, both at home and in China.Article continues below You may like Nvidia prepares shipment of 82,000 AI GPUs to China as chip war lines blur China expected to approve H200 imports in early 2026 Nvidia says H200 demand in China is 'very high' as export licenses near completion With China prioritizing its domestic industry and hesitant to let Nvidia hardware build a monopoly, it didn't seem like H200 could make its way to the region in any significant quantities.H200 may be last-generation, but that doesn't mean it's incapable. It's much more powerful than the H20 AI GPUs Nvidia previously sold to China to curb its access to high-end hardware — around six times as much. Now that H200 is back on the menu, Nvidia has reportedly received a lot of interest from Chinese companies for it.CEO Jensen Huang said earlier this week that Nvidia had received licenses to supply "many customers ​in China" and had received orders from a number of companies. To cater to them, Nvidia was restarting the H200 production line, with Huang himself saying that the "supply chain is getting fired up."This will be exciting news for Nvidia investors, as Nvidia reportedly didn't include the potential revenue from selling H200 to China in its suggested $1 trillion revenue plan for the company in 2027.But Nvidia won't make the largest returns on these sales. Although the Trump administration has approved some sales of H200 chips to China, it comes with a 25% revenue share with the U.S. government. Nvidia will have to pay the fee when the chips arrive in the U.S. from their fabrication facilities for approval, before being re-exported.But H200 isn't the only new(ish) hardware that Nvidia will use to improve its market position in China. The company is also adapting its Groq custom inferencing chips for the market, too.Groq to take on China Groq 3 LPUs were featured as a part of Nvidia's Vera Rubin platform at GTC 2026. (Image credit: Nvidia)Groq is the name of the AI inferencing hardware company Nvidia acquired in late-2025 — not to be confused with Elon Musk and xAI's Grok. Technically, Nvidia just acquired a license to use the Groq technology and hired on all its top staff, but that's an acquisition in every way but name, and it conveniently gets around any anti-trust investigations or regulatory approval. What to read next Jensen says Nvidia has received orders from Chinese customers for H200 GPUs, licenses from US gov't — H200 manufacturing restarting Beijing reportedly limiting H200 purchases to those with ‘special circumstances’ Nvidia's $20 billion Groq IP deal bolsters AI market domination These LPUs are very efficient and capable at handling inferencing, day-to-day AI workloads. Although Nvidia's GPUs are likely to remain the gold standard for training large language models for some time to come, the inferencing market is much more competitive, and especially so in China. Hence why even the mighty Nvidia needed to look outside its own organization to find the competitive edge it needed.Developed by company founder and ex-Google TPU designer Jonathan Ross, Groq's LPUs are being built into Nvidia's Vera Rubin server stacks to deliver a comprehensive AI platform that keeps Nvidia at the core, even when it's the licensed technology that's doing the work.As with its GPUs, Nvidia looks set to adapt its Groq LPUs for China. It doesn't have to downgrade their performance, according to Reuters' sources, but it can't sell Vera Rubin to China, so it may need to find a way to sell the LPUs separately, or bundle them with older GPU technology. Using Groq would keep CUDA relevant in the region, deepening its foothold on developers.Whatever configuration Nvidia ends up with, the company will need to convince Chinese companies to adopt it. Where they don't have homegrown training hardware, Chinese tech giants like Baidu and Huawei are developing their own inferencing chips and have received substantial backing from the Chinese ruling party to accelerate their development. And it's pushing to develop advanced chipmaking tools domestically, too.There's also stiff competition from the likes of Meta, which might be working on the pipe-dream-potential of superintelligent AI, but it's also building its own MTIA inferencing chips, with a long road ahead for future releases. And Amazon and Google have their own custom TPUs, which are very competitive within the field.With that in mind, it's no wonder Nvidia is rushing ahead to take advantage of its latest acqui-hire with Groq. The LPUs designed for the Chinese market are reportedly targeting a May release. With H200s also set to flow into China, Nvidia's market share within the region will undoubtedly rise from its nadir of "0%", a figure Huang touted last fall.Tom's Hardware has reached out to Nvidia for additional comment.
items.author: Jon Martindale
items.titleZh: 随着H200即将流入中国，据报道Groq也蓄势待发——英伟达据称正在准备定制版推理芯片以攻入该市场
items.descriptionZh:
经过数月谈判，中国政府已正式批准英伟达（Nvidia）向中国地区销售其上一代AI数据中心GPU H200。尽管特朗普政府此前已为销售放行，但中国政府的最终批准才是关键。据路透社报道，英伟达正积极准备向中国企业进行大规模销售推广，不仅限于H200，其更看重的是通过收购Groq获得的定制AI推理硬件来开拓市场。

**背景与监管博弈**
此次批准标志着中美芯片贸易紧张局势下的一个阶段性突破。英伟达为遵守美国出口管制，此前专为中国市场推出了性能大幅阉割的H20 GPU。而H200作为面向全球市场的上一代旗舰产品，性能远超H20（约6倍），对中国客户具有强大吸引力。然而，销售过程仍受制于美国政府的附加条件：英伟达须将H200芯片先运至美国接受审核，获批后再出口至中国，并需将销售额的25%作为分成上缴美国政府。

**H200：旧款仍具竞争力**
尽管英伟达已基于Blackwell架构推出新一代B300芯片，并逐步停止了H200的生产线，但由于中国市场的需求以及最终获得的许可，公司已重启H200生产。首席执行官黄仁勋证实已获得向“中国多家客户”供货的许可证，并接到了订单。对于英伟达而言，向中国销售H200带来的收入并未纳入其2027年万亿美元营收的规划中，但这部分增量无疑对投资者是个利好消息。

**核心战略：Groq LPU进军中国推理市场**
英伟达更大的战略意图在于将其通过收购（实质为技术许可和人才吸纳）获得的Groq公司的LPU（语言处理单元）技术打入中国市场。Groq由前谷歌TPU设计师Jonathan Ross创立，其LPU是专为AI推理任务设计的专用芯片（ASIC），在运行已训练好的大模型进行日常推理任务时，能效和性能表现出色。

*   **技术原理与创新**：与通用GPU不同，LPU针对语言模型推理的序列计算模式进行了硬件级优化，能够更高效地处理文本生成等任务，降低延迟和能耗。英伟达已将Groq LPU整合进其Vera Rubin服务器平台，旨在提供一个以英伟达生态为核心的完整AI解决方案。
*   **中国市场适配策略**：由于Vera Rubin平台整体无法对华销售，英伟达需为中国市场设计单独的产品形式，例如单独销售LPU或将其与旧款GPU捆绑。关键在于，此举能巩固CUDA生态在中国开发者中的影响力，从而维持长期市场粘性。

**市场竞争与挑战**
英伟达在开拓中国市场时面临严峻的本地化竞争：
1.  **本土训练芯片替代**：在AI训练领域，虽然英伟达GPU仍是金标准，但中国企业如华为等在积极研发替代方案。
2.  **本土推理芯片崛起**：在更具开放性的推理芯片市场，百度、华为等中国科技巨头正获得政府大力支持，加速研发自有推理芯片。
3.  **全球巨头竞争**：国际市场上面临Meta的MTIA、亚马逊和谷歌的自研TPU等强劲竞争。

**影响与展望**
英伟达双管齐下——通过H200满足市场对高性能训练芯片的存量需求，同时借助Groq LPU抢占高增长的推理芯片市场——旨在扭转其在中国市场份额一度跌至极低点的局面。据报道，针对中国市场的LPU产品目标是在五月发布。随着H200恢复供应和LPU新品的推出，英伟达在中国AI芯片市场的份额预计将从低谷回升。然而，能否成功取决于其产品性能、性价比以及应对本土竞争对手和复杂地缘政治环境的能力。

history.title: Walmart flooded with RTX 40-series GPUs as 50-series remains out of reach for most gamers — retailer slashes up to $480 off last-gen GPUs to offer sensible prices
history.description: Walmart stocks up on Nvidia's GeForce RTX 40-series (codenamed Ada Lovelace) graphics cards amid an AI-driven memory shortage.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Daily tracking of GPU prices and stock for Tom's Hardware's GPU index has revealed a major influx of GeForce RTX 40-series (codename Ada Lovelace) graphics cards at Walmart. While Ada Lovelace cards may have slipped from the very top of the best graphics cards list, they remain competitive, especially at their new, sharply discounted prices. The PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Super Verto's price, for example, has plummeted from $1,501 to just $1,019 in the past 24 hours alone.Check out the PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4080 Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 Super Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4070 XLR8 Gaming Verto deal on WalmartCheck out the PNY GeForce RTX 4060 Ti Verto deal on WalmartFalling victim to the persistent memory shortage, Nvidia’s latest GeForce RTX 50-series (codenamed Blackwell) graphics cards continue to see substantial price hikes, effectively creating a big gap between them and gamers. The surges have shed a positive light on the previous generation of Ada Lovelace-powered graphics cards, which are becoming increasingly attractive options.One might even dare to say that Ada Lovelace is aging like fine wine amid current market dynamics. We wouldn't normally recommend purchasing last-generation graphics cards in normal circumstances. However, the current pricing landscape makes them a compelling alternative for desperate gamers seeking an immediate system upgrade, especially if you're rocking an older 30-series card, for instance. Walmart seems to share the same idea.The major U.S. retailer has significantly increased its inventory of 40-series graphics cards, likely in response to the high prices and limited availability of the newer Blackwell GPUs. New listings show a wide range of Ada Lovelace options from the GeForce RTX 4060 to the GeForce RTX 4080 Super. More importantly, Walmart has significantly lowered prices on Ada Lovelace-powered graphics cards by over $400 on some models. This strategy positions Walmart as the go-to retailer for those seeking value in the current volatile graphics card market.As to the retailers' specific motivations, there are a couple of possible factors. It may be that the AI crunch on PC components is making it harder for retailers to come by RTX 50-series GPUs, forcing them to turn to older models. It's also possible that Walmart has plentiful RTX 50-series stock, but that buyers are simply not parting with their cash because of increased prices. GPU sales generally may also be down as users avoid upgrading or building rigs due to inflated prices of other parts like SSDs or RAM. Whatever the reason, Walmart has decided now is the opportune time to get back into the RTX 40-series business.Nvidia GeForce RTX 40-Series Graphics Card PricingSwipe to scroll horizontallyGraphics CardMarch 18 Lowest U.S. PriceMarch 19 Lowest U.S. PricePrice DifferenceOriginal MSRPGeForce RTX 4090$3,199$3,199N/A$1,599GeForce RTX 4080 Super$1,501$1,019-$482$999GeForce RTX 4080$1,499$1,129-$370$1,199GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super$1,249$799-$450$799GeForce RTX 4070 Ti$796$739-$57$799GeForce RTX 4070 Super$898$579-$319$599GeForce RTX 4070$729$579-$150$549GeForce RTX 4060 Ti 16GBOOSOOSN/A$499GeForce RTX 4060 Ti 8GB$469$379-$90$399GeForce RTX 4060$413$435+$22$299The lowest price on a GeForce RTX 4080 Super yesterday was $1,501, but the influx of these graphics cards at Walmart has set a new lowest price of $1,019, resulting in a savings of $482. It's only $20 over the graphics card's original MSRP. We observed similar price cuts with other models. The GeForce RTX 4070 Ti Super has dropped by $450, finding its original MSRP. Meanwhile, the GeForce RTX 4070 Super is $319 cheaper than yesterday's lowest price and $20 lower than its MSRP.The GeForce RTX 4070 Super is the outlier in the current lineup, as it retails at the same price as the GeForce RTX 4070, at least for today. In contrast, the GeForce RTX 4090 has remained consistently priced with no fluctuations, reflecting its premium status and steady demand. Surprisingly, the GeForce RTX 4060 has bucked expectations, rising in price rather than becoming more budget-friendly. It is just a small example of the unpredictable nature of today's graphics card market.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsFor example, the most affordable GeForce RTX 5070 on the market currently sells for $629, which is 9% more expensive than the GeForce RTX 4070 Super and delivers marginally better performance. There's also the topic of features, though. While Nvidia has expanded some of its technologies backwards to Ada Lovelace, such as DLSS 4.5, some features are only fully enabled on Blackwell. Take Multi-Frame Generation, which runs up to 6X on Blackwell but is restricted to 2X on these cards. A quick glance at our GPU hierarchy shows how these cards stack up against newer models for raw performance:Image 1 of 4(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)Shoppers have to weigh whether Blackwell's performance gains and exclusive gaming features justify its premium. But in the current situation, you can't deny that Ada Lovelace offers good value even if it's a previous-generation product. history.author: Zhiye Liu
history.titleZh: RTX 50系列显卡触不可及，沃尔玛RTX 40系列库存堆积如山——零售商最高降价480美元促销上代显卡
history.descriptionZh:
近期，全球显卡市场出现显著分化，引发消费者关注。Tom's Hardware的每日GPU价格与库存追踪数据显示，美国零售商沃尔玛正在大量补货英伟达基于Ada Lovelace架构的GeForce RTX 40系列显卡，并对其进行了大幅度的价格下调。这一动作与新一代基于Blackwell架构的RTX 50系列显卡因持续的内存短缺和AI领域需求挤压导致的严重供应紧张和价格飙升形成了鲜明对比。市场动态促使上一代产品重现高性价比吸引力。

**市场背景与价格动态**
当前，RTX 50系列显卡的高昂价格和有限供货在玩家与新一代产品之间制造了巨大鸿沟。这一情况反而让已上市的RTX 40系列显卡显得更具吸引力。沃尔玛显然抓住了这一市场机会，大幅增加了RTX 40系列的库存，并对多款型号进行了超过400美元的深度降价，旨在成为当前波动市场中寻求性价比用户的首选零售商。

具体的价格变动数据显示了此次降价的力度：
- **RTX 4080 Super**：最低价在24小时内从1501美元暴跌至1019美元，降幅达482美元，仅比其999美元的官方建议零售价（MSRP）高出20美元。
- **RTX 4070 Ti Super**：价格下降450美元，直接回落至其799美元的官方建议零售价。
- **RTX 4070 Super**：降价319美元，现价579美元，甚至比其599美元的官方建议零售价还低20美元。
- **RTX 4070**：价格降至579美元，与RTX 4070 Super当前价位相同。
- 其他型号如RTX 4080、RTX 4070 Ti等也有显著降价。
值得注意的是，旗舰型号RTX 4090价格保持稳定，体现了其高端市场的稳固需求。而RTX 4060则逆势小幅上涨，凸显了当前显卡市场的不可预测性。

**技术对比与购买考量**
从技术层面看，新一代Blackwell架构（RTX 50系列）虽然带来了性能提升和独家功能，但Ada Lovelace架构（RTX 40系列）仍具备强大竞争力。英伟达已将部分新技术，如DLSS 4.5，向后兼容至Ada Lovelace显卡，但某些核心功能，如“多帧生成”（Multi-Frame Generation），在Blackwell上可实现最高6倍的性能，而在Ada Lovelace上则被限制在2倍。

性能与价格比是当前消费者决策的关键。以市场上最便宜的RTX 5070为例，其当前售价为629美元，比降價后的RTX 4070 Super（579美元）贵约9%，但性能提升幅度有限。因此，对于迫切需要升级系统（特别是仍在使用的30系列或更早显卡的用户）的玩家而言，大幅降价的RTX 40系列提供了一个极具吸引力的替代选择。

**市场动因分析**
零售商此举的背后可能有多重因素：AI热潮对PC组件的挤压使得RTX 50系列GPU采购困难，迫使零售商转向旧型号；也可能沃尔玛虽有RTX 50系列库存，但因价格过高导致消费者购买意愿低迷；此外，SSD、内存等其他部件价格上涨也可能抑制了用户升级或组装整机的需求，导致GPU整体销量下滑。无论如何，沃尔玛判断现在是重新发力RTX 40系列业务的有利时机。

**总结**
在当前的市场环境下，尽管RTX 40系列已非最新一代，但其大幅降价后的价格与仍属主流的性能相结合，提供了出色的性价比。对于大多数游戏玩家来说，在Blackwell显卡价格回归理性之前，降价的Ada Lovelace显卡是一个务实且高价值的选择。沃尔玛的库存策略和定价调整，精准回应了由供应链问题和AI需求外溢导致的消费级显卡市场结构性变化。

history.titleZh: 宏碁掠夺者Helios Neo 16S AI笔记本直降350美元——配备OLED屏与32GB内存的最低价RTX 5070 Ti机型现售1549美元
history.descriptionZh:
在当前个人电脑市场中，组装台式机的内存等组件价格居高不下，使得游戏笔记本电脑成为愈发吸引人的选择，尤其对潜在的首台电脑组装者而言。我们注意到一款此类设备的优惠信息：宏碁（Acer）Predator Helios Neo 16S AI 游戏笔记本正在 Best Buy 限时促销，售价仅为 1,549 美元，较原价直降 350 美元。

这款笔记本不仅是该型号的历史最低价，同时也是市面上配备 OLED 显示屏的 RTX 5070 Ti 笔记本电脑中价格最优惠的机型。其核心硬件配置堪称旗舰级别：搭载了英特尔酷睿 Ultra 9 275HX 处理器，拥有 24 个核心，最高睿频可达 5.4 GHz，配合 32 GB 的 DDR5-6400 高速内存，足以流畅应对任何生产力任务。

显示方面，它采用了一块 16 英寸、刷新率高达 240 Hz 的 OLED 面板，支持 G-Sync 技术，并实现了 100% 的 DCI-P3 色域覆盖，能提供色彩鲜艳、响应迅速的视觉体验。

当进行游戏、渲染或视频编辑时，得益于 Helios 系列的散热系统，功耗 115W 的 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti 独立显卡能够释放出强劲性能。尽管高负载下风扇噪音较为明显，且内置扬声器不足以掩盖此噪声，但所有评测均指出其实际性能表现异常出色。就游戏本而言，它的设计相对轻薄，重量约 5 磅（约 2.27 公斤），厚度不足 19 毫米。

输入/输出接口配置全面且实用，包括多个高速 USB Type-A 和 Type-C 端口、耳机插孔、以太网口、HDMI 接口以及一个 microSD 读卡器。无线连接方面，支持蓝牙 5.4 和 Wi-Fi 6E。其内置的 76 Wh 电池可支持接近全天的移动使用。

总体而言，以 1,549 美元的价格，就能获得如此性能规格的宏碁 Predator Helios Neo 16S AI 游戏笔记本，无疑是一个非常超值的选择。文章最后还建议，若读者希望寻找更多优惠，可以参考网站的其他硬件deal页面，例如 SSD 和存储设备、硬盘、游戏显示器、显卡、游戏椅、最佳 Wi-Fi 路由器、主板或 CPU 等专项优惠。

history.title: How Nvidia's $20 billion Groq 3 LPU deal reshapes the Nvidia Vera Rubin Platform — Samsung 4nm process serves as bedrock for SRAM-based AI accelerator chip
history.description: Nvidia unveiled the Groq 3 language processing unit at GTC 2026 in San Jose on Monday, marking the first chip to emerge from its $20 billion licensing and talent deal with Groq.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia unveiled the Groq 3 language processing unit at GTC 2026 in San Jose on Monday, marking the first chip to emerge from its $20 billion licensing and talent deal with AI inference startup Groq, which was struck on Christmas Eve last year. The SRAM-based inference accelerator slots into the Vera Rubin platform as a dedicated decode-phase co-processor, and Nvidia plans to ship it in Q3 2026, manufactured by Samsung on a 4nm process. It is the company's first rack-scale product built around non-GPU silicon — and its arrival has already displaced a homegrown Nvidia chip from the roadmap.The LP30 chip at the heart of the Groq 3 LPX rack carries 512 MB of on-chip SRAM per die, delivering 150 TB/s of memory bandwidth. That figure dwarfs the 22 TB/s available from the 288 GB of HBM4 on each Rubin GPU. A full LPX rack houses 256 LPUs for a total of 128GB of SRAM and 40 PB/s of aggregate bandwidth. Nvidia claims the LPX rack, paired with a Vera Rubin NVL72, delivers 35 times higher throughput per megawatt than Blackwell NVL72 alone for trillion-parameter models, at a target price point of $45 per million tokens.Groq 3 and Vera Rubin Nvidia detailed its entire seven-chip Rubin SuperPOD strategy at GTC 2026. (Image credit: Nvidia)Rubin GPUs handle the compute-intensive prefill phase of a query, processing long input contexts, while Groq LPUs take over the decode phase, generating output tokens at low latency. Nvidia's Dynamo orchestration platform manages the split across heterogeneous hardware, distributing workloads based on batch size and parallelism requirements.Article continues below You may like Nvidia Groq 3 LPU and Groq LPX racks join Rubin platform at GTC — SRAM-packed accelerator boosts 'every layer of the AI model on every token' Nvidia's $20 billion Groq IP deal bolsters AI market domination Nvidia removes Rubin CPX accelerators from its roadmap The original, pre-Nvidia Groq LPU design used a fixed Very Long Instruction Word (VLIW) pipeline and large on-chip SRAM pools, with the compiler pre-scheduling the entire execution path at compile time, which meant deterministic latency with no cache misses or stalls. These chips also demonstrated raw single-user token rates in the thousands per second, but the architecture's weakness was always capacity. At 230MB of SRAM per chip in prior generations, fitting even medium-sized models required high chip counts, and the architecture was initially designed for convolutional neural networks.The Groq LP30 addresses some of these limitations with 512 MB of SRAM per die and 1.23 FP8 PFLOPS of compute capability. Samsung has ramped production from roughly 9,000 wafers to about 15,000 wafers as output shifts from samples to commercial manufacturing, with AWS announcing at GTC that it will deploy Groq 3 LPUs alongside more than one million Nvidia GPUs as part of an expanded partnership.Beyond the LP30, a future LP35 will add NVFP4 support, aligning with the Rubin Ultra generation, and an LP40 is planned for the Feynman architecture cycle after that.Rubin CPX axed?One conspicuous absence from GTC was the Rubin CPX, a GDDR7-based inference accelerator announced in September 2025 as part of the Vera Rubin platform; it was absent from all keynote slides and received no stage time. It appears — though not officially confirmed — that the CPX has been removed from Nvidia’s roadmap entirely, replaced in the platform hierarchy by Groq 3 LPX.Rubin CPX was designed to use cheaper, more available GDDR7 memory to accelerate the context phase of inference at lower power. But the Groq LPU offers higher bandwidth without requiring large quantities of any external memory, which is ideal in a market where HBM supply remains constrained, and GDDR7 production is still scaling. Off-roadmap parts could still ship to customers who have already invested in CPX software optimization, but there’s a clear shift in priorities at Nvidia.There’s also an uncanny comparison between this and the Mellanox acquisition in 2019. That ended up turning Nvidia’s NVLink and InfiniBand technologies into foundational infrastructure for AI clusters. Groq appears to be following a similar trajectory, whereby start-up tech is absorbed into the platform as a permanent new architectural layer.Inference chip consolidationNvidia's Groq deal is the largest in a wave of inference-focused acquisitions that swept through the semiconductor industry in 2025. In June, AMD acquired the engineering team from Untether AI, a RISC-V inference chip developer, after the startup shut down, and Nvidia itself paid over $900 million for networking startup Enfabrica's team and IP in September. Meta acquired custom-chip startup Rivos in October, and Intel attempted to buy SambaNova for a reported $1.6 billion, but the talks collapsed; the two companies settled on a $350 million investment and multi-year partnership last month instead. What to read next Examining Nvidia's 60 exaflop Vera Rubin POD — how seven chips underpin company's 40 rack AI factory supercomputer Nvidia buys AI chip rival Groq's IP for $20 billion in its biggest deal ever Nvidia launches Vera Rubin NVL72 AI supercomputer at CES There’s a consistent pattern here, where independent inference chip startups are being absorbed by large incumbents, and the economics of competing independently against Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem have proven unsustainable regardless of technical merit. Groq itself was targeting $500 million in revenue for the fiscal year 2025, but even that wasn’t enough to sustain independence. Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon noted in a research report that the deal's non-exclusive licensing structure may keep "the fiction of competition alive" while effectively neutralizing a rival.Hyperscaler custom silicon Meta outlined its MTIA AI accelerator roadmap just last week. (Image credit: Meta)While startups consolidate into incumbents, the hyperscalers are building their own inference hardware at an accelerating pace.Meta announced four successive MTIA chip generations on March 11, all developed in partnership with Broadcom: the MTIA 300 (already in production for ranking and recommendation training), MTIA 400 (completing lab testing), MTIA 450, and MTIA 500, with the latter two targeted at generative AI inference and scheduled for mass deployment in 2027. The company has already deployed hundreds of thousands of earlier MTIA chips for inference across its apps. From MTIA 300 to 500, HBM bandwidth increases 4.5 times, and compute FLOPS increase 25 times.Google's Ironwood TPU v7 delivers 4,614 TFLOPS per chip with 192 GB of HBM per die, scaling to 42.5 exaflops in 9,216-chip pods. AWS continues developing Trainium and Inferentia, though internal data reported in 2024 showed Trainium at just 0.5% of Nvidia GPU usage within AWS and Inferentia at 2.7%, suggesting adoption has lagged.Spending figures also demonstrate this diversification, with a Futurum Group survey from November 2025 finding that XPU accelerators are expected to lead data center compute spending growth at 22% in 2026, outpacing GPUs at 19% and CPUs at 14%. Meanwhile, TrendForce projects custom ASIC shipments from cloud providers growing 44.6% in 2026, compared to 16.1% growth for GPU shipments.Nvidia's response is to make sure its platform includes non-GPU silicon before someone else's does, and the Groq 3 LPU is the product of that. Meanwhile, the future of Rubin CPX appears uncertain, at least for now.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 英伟达200亿美元Groq 3 LPU交易如何重构Vera Rubin平台——三星4纳米工艺为SRAM架构AI加速芯片奠定基石
history.descriptionZh:
在2026年3月于圣何塞举行的GTC大会上，英伟达正式发布了Groq 3语言处理单元，这是该公司自去年圣诞节前夕以200亿美元收购AI推理初创公司Groq的知识产权和人才后推出的首款芯片产品。这款基于SRAM的推理加速器将作为专用解码阶段协处理器，集成于Vera Rubin平台。英伟达计划于2026年第三季度开始出货，芯片由三星采用4纳米工艺制造。这是英伟达首款围绕非GPU芯片构建的机架级产品，它的出现甚至导致英伟达自家的一款芯片从路线图中被移除。

**核心技术原理与架构创新**

Groq 3 LPU的核心是LP30芯片，每颗裸片集成512 MB的片上SRAM，提供高达150 TB/s的内存带宽。这一数字远超每颗Rubin GPU上288 GB HBM4所能提供的22 TB/s带宽。一个完整的LPX机架容纳256颗LPU，总计拥有128GB SRAM和40 PB/s的聚合带宽。

其工作流程体现了异构计算分工：Rubin GPU负责处理计算密集型的预填充阶段，解析长输入上下文；而Groq LPU则接管解码阶段，以低延迟生成输出词元。英伟达的Dynamo编排平台负责管理这种异构硬件间的任务分割，根据批量大小和并行性需求分发工作负载。

此次发布的Groq LP30芯片是对原始Groq LPU设计的重大演进。原始设计采用固定的超长指令字流水线和大型片上SRAM池，编译器在编译时预先调度整个执行路径，从而实现确定性延迟，避免了缓存未命中或停滞。该架构曾展示出每秒数千词元的原始单用户吞吐率，但其核心弱点始终是容量。前几代芯片每颗仅230MB SRAM，即使部署中等规模模型也需要大量芯片，且其架构最初是为卷积神经网络设计的。

LP30通过将每裸片SRAM增至512 MB，并具备1.23 FP8 PFLOPS的计算能力，部分解决了这些限制。三星已将其产量从约9000片晶圆提升至约15000片，标志着生产从样品阶段转向商业制造。AWS在GTC上宣布，作为扩大合作的一部分，将在其云平台部署Groq 3 LPU， alongside 超过一百万颗英伟达GPU。

未来，英伟达还规划了支持NVFP4、与Rubin Ultra世代对齐的LP35，以及面向后续Feynman架构周期的LP40。

**性能参数与市场定位**

英伟达声称，一个LPX机架与一个Vera Rubin NVL72配对，在运行万亿参数模型时，每兆瓦的吞吐量比单独的Blackwell NVL72高出35倍，目标定价为每百万词元45美元。

这一性能提升主要得益于SRAM架构带来的极高带宽和低延迟，尤其适合对延迟敏感的大模型推理场景。

**产品路线图调整与行业影响**

一个显著的变动是，此前于2025年9月作为Vera Rubin平台一部分发布的、基于GDDR7内存的推理加速器Rubin CPX，在本次GTC大会的主题演讲和幻灯片中完全消失。虽然没有官方确认，但迹象表明CPX可能已完全从英伟达路线图中移除，被Groq 3 LPX所取代。

Rubin CPX原本旨在利用更便宜、更易获取的GDDR7内存，以更低功耗加速推理的上下文阶段。但Groq LPU提供了更高的带宽，且无需依赖大量外部内存，这在HBM供应持续紧张、GDDR7产能仍在爬升的市场中显得更为理想。尽管已投资CPX软件优化的客户可能仍会获得供货，但英伟达的优先级发生了明确转变。

这一收购整合模式令人联想到英伟达2019年对Mellanox的收购，后者最终将NVLink和InfiniBand技术打造成了AI集群的基础设施。Groq似乎正沿着相似的轨迹发展，其初创技术被吸收进平台，成为一个永久性的新架构层。

**AI推理芯片行业整合趋势**

英伟达收购Groq是2025年半导体行业一波以推理为核心的收购潮中规模最大的一笔。同年，AMD收购了已关闭的RISC-V推理芯片开发商Untether AI的工程团队；英伟达以超9亿美元收购了网络初创公司Enfabrica的团队和IP；Meta收购了定制芯片初创公司Rivos；英特尔曾试图以约16亿美元收购SambaNova，但谈判破裂，最终双方于上月达成了3.5亿美元的投资和多年期合作伙伴关系。

这一系列事件呈现出清晰模式：独立的推理芯片初创公司正被大型现有企业吸收。无论技术优势如何，独立对抗英伟达CUDA生态系统的经济模式已被证明难以为继。Groq本身在2025财年目标营收为5亿美元，但这仍不足以维持其独立运营。正如伯恩斯坦分析师Stacy Rasgon指出的，此次交易的非独家授权结构可能在“维持竞争表象”的同时，有效消除了一位竞争对手。

**超大规模企业的自定义芯片竞赛**

在初创公司被整合的同时，超大规模云服务商正在加速构建自己的推理硬件。

Meta于3月11日公布了与博通合作开发的四代MTIA AI加速器路线图：已用于排名和推荐训练的MTIA 300、已完成实验室测试的MTIA 400，以及针对生成式AI推理、计划于2027年大规模部署的MTIA 450和MTIA 500。该公司已在旗下应用中部署了数十万颗早期的MTIA芯片进行推理。从MTIA 300到500，HBM带宽增加了4.5倍，计算FLOPS提升了25倍。

谷歌的Ironwood TPU v7每芯片提供4,614 TFLOPS算力，每裸片配备192 GB HBM，可扩展至9216芯片集群，达42.5百亿亿次浮点运算。AWS则继续开发Trainium和Inferentia，不过2024年的内部数据显示，Trainium在AWS内部的GPU使用量中仅占0.5%，Inferentia占2.7%，表明其采用率相对滞后。

支出数据也印证了这种多元化趋势。Futurum Group于2025年11月的一项调查发现，预计2026年数据中心计算支出增长将由XPU加速器引领，增幅达22%，超过GPU的19%和CPU的14%。同时，TrendForce预计，2026年云提供商的自定义ASIC出货量将增长44.6%，而GPU出货量增长为16.1%。

英伟达的应对策略是确保自己的平台率先纳入非GPU芯片，Groq 3 LPU正是这一战略的产物。与此同时，Rubin CPX的未来，至少目前看来，已充满不确定性。这场由大模型推理需求驱动的芯片架构革新与行业格局重塑，正在持续加速演进。

history.title: AMD and Samsung ink memory supply memorandum for EPYC and Instinct products — unprecedented deal also includes scope for foundry partnership
history.description: Samsung to remain primary HBM memory supplier for AMD's AI accelerators as the companies look into possible foundry relationship.

Subscribe to our newsletter AMD and Samsung this week signed a rather unprecedented memorandum of understanding (MOU) that involves the strategic supply of memory for AMD's next-generation EPYC and Instinct MI455X products, as well as discussions of a potential foundry partnership. The MOU aims to ensure that AMD gets enough memory for its next-generation CPU and AI accelerator products throughout their life span, whereas Samsung will get a chance to serve as a foundry for a leading supplier of CPUs, AI GPUs, and other products.Samsung has been the primary supplier of HBM3E for AMD's Instinct MI350X and MI355X AI accelerators, so it is not surprising that the core of the new agreement is Samsung’s role as a primary supplier of HBM4 memory for AMD's upcoming Instinct MI455X accelerator. As demand for all types of memory in general and HBM4 in particular exceeds supply, AMD must ensure that its next-generation MI455X accelerator (and possibly other Instinct MI400-series products) gets enough memory.AMD's Instinct MI455X is projected to use 12 HBM4 12-Hi memory stacks for a total of 432 GB. Given such demands, it makes great sense for AMD to sign a strategic memory supply deal, though for now the two companies limited themselves to the MOU. Meanwhile, Samsung said that its HBM4 memory stacks are based on memory devices produced using its 1c (6th Generation 10nm-class) process technology and use a base die made on a 4nm-class logic process, which enables them to achieve a rather unprecedented data transfer rate of 13 GT/s (thus provide up to 3.3 TB/s of bandwidth per stack), which is well above JEDEC's recommended 8 GT/s for HBM4. Yet, memory speeds supported by Instinct MI455X are considerably slower.Article continues below You may like Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron team up to block memory hoarding Micron secures $318 million Taiwanese subsidy for HBM R&D as AI memory arms race intensifies Intel is co-developing new Z-Angle Memory to compete with HBM used in AI data centers In addition to HBM4, the MOU between the two companies also includes DDR5 supply for AMD's 6th Generation EPYC processors codenamed 'Venice,' as well as AMD's Helios rack-scale systems for AI data centers that rely on EPYC 'Venice' and Instinct MI455X. Again, the move aims to ensure a steady memory supply for next-generation AI systems running AMD's CPUs and AI accelerators to ensure a competitive position against Nvidia's Rubin-powered machines.Perhaps the most intriguing part of the announcement is that the MOU can open the door for a potential foundry relationship, under which Samsung could manufacture future AMD products. While no specific nodes or chips were disclosed, this signals a willingness to expand cooperation into logic production alongside memory supply. To put this into context, AMD has been drifting away from GlobalFoundries since late 2018, and by now virtually all of its advanced products are made at TSMC. Shifting certain advanced production to Samsung is costly, though if this ensures that the South Korean company will provide much-needed memory to support AMD's AI efforts, then it looks like the developer of EPYC CPUs and Instinct accelerators may be willing to adopt a dual-source foundry strategy and make at least some of its products at Samsung Foundry."Samsung and AMD share a commitment to advancing AI computing, and this agreement reflects the growing scope of our collaboration," said Young Hyun Jun, Vice Chairman & CEO of Samsung Electronics. "From industry-leading HBM4 and next-generation memory architectures to cutting-edge foundry and advanced packaging, Samsung is uniquely positioned to deliver unrivaled turnkey capabilities that support AMD’s evolving AI roadmap." history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: AMD与三星签署EPYC和Instinct产品存储供应备忘录——此次前所未有的协议还涵盖代工合作可能
history.descriptionZh:
本周，AMD与三星签署了一份意义深远的谅解备忘录（MOU），其核心内容包括为AMD下一代EPYC处理器及Instinct MI455X加速器提供战略性内存供应，并探讨潜在代工合作伙伴关系。此举旨在确保AMD下一代CPU和AI加速器产品在整个生命周期内获得充足的内存供应，而三星则有望成为这家领先CPU、AI GPU供应商的代工合作伙伴。

作为AMD Instinct MI350X和MI355X AI加速器HBM3E内存的主要供应商，三星在此次新协议中继续扮演关键角色，将成为AMD即将推出的Instinct MI455X加速器所需HBM4内存的主要供应商。当前，各类内存（尤其是HBM4）需求远超供应，AMD必须确保其下一代MI455X加速器（可能还包括其他Instinct MI400系列产品）获得足够的内存。预计MI455X将采用12个HBM4 12-Hi内存堆栈，总容量达432GB。面对如此巨大的需求，AMD签署战略性内存供应协议合情合理，不过目前双方仅停留在谅解备忘录阶段。

三星表示，其HBM4内存堆栈基于采用1c（第六代10nm级）工艺技术生产的存储芯片，并使用了4nm级逻辑工艺制造的基板芯片。这使得其数据传输速率达到了前所未有的13 GT/s（因此每堆栈可提供高达3.3 TB/s的带宽），远超JEDEC为HBM4推荐的8 GT/s标准。然而，Instinct MI455X实际支持的内存速度要慢得多。

除了HBM4，此次谅解备忘录还包括为AMD代号“威尼斯”的第六代EPYC处理器，以及依赖EPYC “威尼斯”和Instinct MI455X的AMD赫利奥斯AI数据中心机架级系统供应DDR5内存。此举同样是为了确保运行AMD CPU和AI加速器的下一代AI系统拥有稳定的内存供应，从而在与英伟达基于Rubin平台的系统的竞争中保持有利地位。

此次声明中最引人关注的部分或许是，该谅解备忘录可能为潜在的代工合作关系打开大门，即三星未来可能为AMD制造产品。尽管未披露具体的工艺节点或芯片，但这表明双方有意愿将合作从内存供应扩展到逻辑芯片制造领域。回溯背景，AMD自2018年底以来已逐渐与格芯疏远，目前其几乎所有先进产品均由台积电制造。将部分先进生产转向三星成本高昂，但如果此举能确保这家韩国公司为AMD的AI战略提供急需的内存，那么这位EPYC CPU和Instinct加速器的开发商似乎愿意采用双源代工策略，并至少在三星代工厂生产部分产品。

三星电子副董事长兼首席执行官Young Hyun Jun表示：“三星和AMD都致力于推进AI计算，此协议反映了我们不断扩大的合作范围。从行业领先的HBM4和下一代内存架构，到尖端的代工和先进封装技术，三星拥有独特的优势，能够提供全面的交钥匙能力，支持AMD不断演进的AI路线图。”

history.title: Kioxia announces new Super High IOPS SSD that helps accelerate AI workloads on Nvidia GPUs — 25.6TB drive provides more GPU-accessible memory for faster data access
history.description: Kioxia has developed a new AI SSD that is designed to provide a secondary cache for Nvidia AI GPUs. The new drive comes with the manufacturer's XL-Flash that is SLC based and performs at over 10 million IOPS.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia and Kioxia have been working on a new SSD design that will keep Nvidia's AI GPUs fed with no downtime during intensive AI workloads. Kioxia has announced the new E3.S CM9 series, which Kioxia classifies as a "Super High IOPS" SSD that offers 25.6TB of capacity and can write to itself three times per day for the entirety of its warranty period. The new drive will be available for customers to evaluate by the end of 2026.The new drive is part of Kioxia and Nvidia's move to bring incredibly fast storage to AI GPUs. The CM9 series comes with Kioxia's XL-flash, which is designed to achieve over 10 million IOPS, a figure that is around three to four times greater than traditional datacenter SSDs. XL-Flash is made using SLC NAND flash (the fastest flash type available) that boasts a read latency range of just 3 to 5 microseconds. By comparison, traditional SSDs normally have a peak at 3 to 4 million IOPS and have read latencies in the 40 to 100-microsecond range.These new drives are part of Nvidia's Storage-Next design that sees servers utilize these drives in conjunction with a direct link to the GPU itself, bypassing additional latency penalties that would occur when transferring data from the CPU. This technology is designed to offset the limitations of existing HBM memory, giving Nvidia's AI GPUs a secondary cache layer to store data and keep the GPU cores operating at 100% without any downtime. This addresses the growing problem of growing AI models that are scaling towards trillions of parameters and context windows that consist of millions of tokens.Article continues below You may like First PCIe 6.0 SSD enters mass production with 28GB/s speeds, 5.5 million IOPS, and liquid cooling SK hynix and SanDisk announce speedy HBF flash chip standard Phison demos 10X faster AI inference on consumer PCs with software and hardware combo that enables 3x larger AI models We first saw this technology with the BlueField-4 STX storage architecture at GTC 2026. BlueField-4 uses a storage-optimized BlueField-4 DPU and ConnectX-9 SuperNIC that Nvidia claims delivers up to five times the token throughput, four times better energy efficiency, and twice the page ingestion speed compared to traditional CPU-based storage architectures. history.author: Aaron Klotz
history.titleZh: 铠侠发布新款超高IOPS固态硬盘 加速英伟达GPU人工智能负载——25.6TB容量提供更多GPU可访问内存 实现更快数据读取
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达与铠侠正合作开发一款新型SSD，旨在为高强度AI工作负载中的英伟达AI GPU提供持续无间断的数据供给。铠侠近日宣布了E3.S CM9系列，该系列被归类为“超高IOPS”SSD，提供25.6TB容量，并保证在其整个保修期内每天可完成三次全盘写入。新驱动器预计将于2026年底可供客户评估。

这款新驱动器是铠侠与英伟达推动为AI GPU提供极速存储解决方案的一部分。CM9系列搭载了铠侠的XL-Flash技术，旨在实现超过1000万IOPS，这一数字约为传统数据中心SSD的三到四倍。XL-Flash采用SLC NAND闪存（目前最快的闪存类型），其读取延迟范围仅为3至5微秒。相比之下，传统SSD的峰值IOPS通常在300万至400万，读取延迟在40至100微秒范围内。

这些新驱动器属于英伟达“Storage-Next”设计的一部分。在该设计中，服务器将这些驱动器与GPU本身直接链接，绕过了通过CPU传输数据时产生的额外延迟开销。此项技术旨在弥补现有HBM内存的容量限制，为英伟达的AI GPU提供一个二级缓存层来存储数据，从而确保GPU核心能够保持100%利用率，无需任何停机等待。这解决了日益增长的AI模型规模所带来的难题——模型参数量正朝向万亿级发展，上下文窗口也包含数百万个令牌。

我们最早在2026年GTC大会上通过BlueField-4 STX存储架构见证了此项技术。BlueField-4采用了一个面向存储优化的BlueField-4 DPU和ConnectX-9 SuperNIC。英伟达宣称，与传统的基于CPU的存储架构相比，该架构可提供高达5倍的令牌吞吐量、4倍的能效提升以及2倍的页面摄取速度。

history.title: Intel's new feature can improve game loading times by up to 3x — Precompiled Shader Delivery comes to Arc Xe2 and Xe3 GPUs following DirectX SDK release
history.description: Following in Nvidia's footsteps, Intel has now officially adopted Microsoft's Advanced Shader Delivery to make shader compilation much faster in games. Intel is calling it Precompiled Shader Distribution and it's available on a bunch of Arc GPUs right away, supported in 11 games at launch, with more likely to follow. AMD is now the only company left who hasn't officially embraced this feature.

Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft launched Advanced Shader Delivery (ASD) last year as part of the DirectX SDK, but it was limited to only the ROG Xbox Ally devices at the time. The feature was always intended to eventually go mainstream — and Intel has just released a new graphics driver that brings a version of it to Arc GPUs. The company is calling it "Precompiled Shader Distribution" and it's available on select mobile and desktop chips.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCPrecompiled Shader Distribution downloads the shaders for a game ahead of time, so you don't have to wait for the shader cache to build up when you first open it. The process happens automatically in the background once the Intel Graphics app detects you have the game installed. Depending on your exact hardware config, it starts downloading shaders from Intel's cloud and loads them into a dedicated folder.Intel told TechPowerUp it is "also working with Microsoft on launching Advanced Shader Delivery later this year," meaning that this is Intel's own implementation for now — it will only get better from here (hopefully). The feature is launching with 13 Steam games: They're all heavy-hitters and will offer some level of faster loading, while reducing shader stuttering mid-gameplay.Black Myth: WukongBorderlands 4Call of Duty: Black Ops 6Call of Duty: Black Ops 7Cyberpunk 2077God of War RagnarökGotham KnightsHogwarts LegacyNBA 2K26StarfieldS.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2: Heart of ChornobylThe Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion RemasteredThe Outer Worlds 2The company is claiming this will make your games load 2x faster on Arc B-series discrete GPUs and on the Core Ultra 200 series' integrated graphics, on average. The latest Panther Lake lineup with Xe3 graphics will benefit even more and will receive an average 3x improvement in game loading times. There's no support for Arc Alchemist yet — and it may not be coming soon, since Intel lists the latest Arc GPUs as a hardware requirement.Article continues below You may like Shader Execution Reordering part of new DirectX update boosts FPS by 90% in Intel Arc B-series GPUs Intel enables XeSS 3 Multi-Frame Generation in latest drivers, expanding frame generation across Arc GPUs and Core Ultra iGPUs Intel releases XeSS 3.0 SDK with 3x and 4x multi-frame gen support, but it's still not open source In the benchmarks shared, God of War: Ragnarok was the most impressive by far, loading an insane 37 times faster with Precompiled Shader Distribution on an Arc B390 iGPU. The Oblivion Remaster only saw a 1.3x improvement across both the Arc B580 graphics card and the Arc 140V iGPU on a Core Ultra 9 288V. Of course, not every game will take full advantage of this feature — especially not right away.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Intel)(Image credit: Intel)(Image credit: Intel)But as Advanced Shader Delivery becomes commonplace and other chipmakers like Nvidia and AMD started introducing it on the driver level, shader caching issues should slowly become a thing of the past. PC will be able to achieve parity with console in yet another field, since its plus point — the variety of hardware configs — has actually held back precompiled shaders from being a possibility on the platform for a long time.Precompiled Shader Delivery will also update cache for all the shaders alongside new drivers, so your game is always running smoothly. Within the driver release, Intel actually calls this feature "Graphics Shader Distribution Service" — but it refers to the same thing. If you want to enable it on your own Arc GPU, open the Intel Graphics Software, go to Graphics, then 3D Rendering, and you'll see the toggle right there.
